Document:
# Comms Quick Reference
A compact reference for radio controls, ranges, and status effects.
For detailed instructions, see [textlink="AN/PRC-117G Radio" link="AU14CommsANPRC"] and [textlink="Field Headsets" link="AU14CommsHeadsets"].
## Chat Prefixes
[bold]Prefix[/bold]
[bold]Function[/bold]
[color=#a4885c]:h[/color]
Transmit on the headset's default net.
[color=#a4885c]:v[/color], [color=#a4885c]:a[/color], and others
Transmit on another keyed headset net. Examine the headset to view its available prefixes.
[color=#a4885c]:r[/color]
Transmit through the active AN/PRC slot or handset.
[color=#a4885c]:x[/color]
Transmit on a headset's tuned direct frequency.
The AN/PRC panel can be operated by:
- Radio Traffic Operators - Commanders - Officer Adjutants - Advisors - Platoon Sergeants - CLF Cell Leaders
The handset can be used by anyone within range.
## Signal Range
The following AN/PRC ranges assume [bold]TX MED[/bold] and a whip antenna.
[bold]System[/bold]
[bold]Clear range[/bold]
[bold]Degraded or lost[/bold]
Named net through AN/PRC
0-30 tiles from the radio.
Degraded to 45 tiles. No signal beyond 45.
Direct frequency
0-30 tiles from the transmitter.
Degraded to 50 tiles. No signal beyond 50.
AN/PRC frequency bridge
Both users within 50 tiles of the radio.
Fails when either user is outside bridge range.
Command-post antenna mast
0-75 tiles.
Degraded to 100 tiles. No signal beyond 100.
Shipboard communications array
Covers the connected ship while powered.
Provides no coverage when destroyed or unpowered.
Handset cord
Approximately two tiles from the radio.
Disconnects when the user moves out of range.
Transmit power and antenna type modify AN/PRC relay range.
Faction nets, including squad, command, intelligence, JTAC, and MP, require coverage from at least one of the following:
- A powered AN/PRC worn by a trained operator. - A deployed retransmission station. - A command-post antenna mast. - A powered communications array. - A vehicle radio set aboard a dropship or vehicle (covers little more than the hull). - A field antenna mast, raised from ten metal sheets by engineering-trained personnel.
Colony and civilian channels are not relay-gated and keep stock behavior.
## Antennas
[bold]Antenna[/bold]
[bold]Operation[/bold]
[bold]Range[/bold]
[bold]WHIP[/bold]
Mobile or stationary.
Standard range.
[bold]MAST[/bold]
Best while stationary.
Approximately double range while stationary and half standard range while moving.
## Operating Modes
[bold]Mode[/bold]
[bold]Function[/bold]
[bold]Benefits[/bold]
[bold]Limitations[/bold]
[bold]FH[/bold]
Frequency-hopping traffic.
Reduces jamming by one tier and permits transmission inside jammer fields.
Uses 1.5× battery and creates a small direction-finding signature.
[bold]SC[/bold]
Normal secured traffic.
Uses 0.75× battery and creates no direction-finding signature while secured.
Provides no jamming resistance.
[bold]CT[/bold]
Cipher traffic between AN/PRC radios.
Hidden from receivers without matching fill and creates no direction-finding signature.
Requires fill-loaded AN/PRCs and provides no jamming resistance.
[bold]PT[/bold]
Plain-text broadcast.
Uses 0.5× battery.
Readable by any compatible receiver, increases jamming by one tier, and creates the largest direction-finding risk.
Matching COMSEC fill secures FH, SC, and CT traffic. PT traffic is always sent in the clear.
## Transmit Power
[bold]TX[/bold]
[bold]Range[/bold]
[bold]Battery use[/bold]
[bold]DF signature[/bold]
[bold]LO[/bold]
0.6×
0.5×
0.5×
[bold]MED[/bold]
1×
1×
1×
[bold]HI[/bold]
1.5×
2×
2×
## Squelch
Signal quality falls off smoothly with distance from the nearest relay: clear close in, light hiss over the outer band, broken fragments at the fringe. Squelch decides how much of that damaged traffic you hear - it never repairs a transmission, it only mutes it.
[bold]SQL[/bold]
[bold]Filtering[/bold]
[bold]0[/bold]
Passes all traffic, including heavy static.
[bold]1[/bold]
Suppresses heavy static and undecodable encrypted traffic.
[bold]2[/bold]
Suppresses medium jammer degradation.
[bold]3[/bold]
Default. Suppresses light jammer degradation and broken fringe-range traffic.
[bold]4[/bold]
Maximum filtering. Even lightly hissy long-range traffic is suppressed.
Lower SQL settings allow more degraded traffic to be heard. Raising squelch buys quiet at the price of missing weak distant stations entirely.
## COMSEC Controls
[bold]Action[/bold]
[bold]Result[/bold]
Insert fill card
Secures compatible faction traffic in FH, SC, and CT modes.
[bold]ZEROIZE[/bold]
Ejects the loaded card without damaging it.
[bold]DESTROY[/bold]
Permanently removes the loaded card.
[bold]RECRYPTO[/bold]
Makes all older cards from that faction stale, including captured cards. Command authority only: Commander, Adjutant, Advisor.
Captured current card
Allows compatible secured traffic from that faction to be decoded until recrypto.
Replacement fill cards are obtained through the normal supply system.
## Direction Finding
The following traffic creates a direction-finding signature, from highest to lowest risk:
- PT traffic. - Faction-net traffic sent without matching fill. - Secured FH traffic.
Secured SC and CT traffic do not create a direction-finding signature.
Repeated signature-producing transmissions from the same location increase the chance of detection. Direction-finding risk decreases after approximately one minute without another qualifying transmission.
[bold]RADIO CHECK[/bold] reports which stations receive the active net clearly or with degradation.
When used inside a jammer field, it also reports a bearing to the strongest jammer.
